title: search — field classification
description: Every column across search_indexes, search_sync_jobs, search_synonyms, search_saved_queries, search_analytics_events classified per foundation's tier taxonomy. Note that DOCUMENT CONTENT stored in the physical engine indexes (Meilisearch, PG FTS, pgvector) inherits the source entity's classification — an athletes index contains regulated_minor rows because Athlete rows are regulated_minor — not the classification of these ledger tables. Enforcement of the source classification (masking restricted-tier fields before indexing) is done by `HasSearchable::toSearchableArray()`, not by this table.

audit_log: SearchSyncJob status transitions are dual-written to activity_log (product feed) and audits (compliance evidence) via HasActivityLog and HasAudit traits.
SearchIndex mutations (registered / unregistered / reindexed / alias-swapped) write to activity_log + audits.
SearchSynonym CRUD (create / update / delete / share) writes to activity_log only — workspace business terminology is not compliance evidence.
SearchSavedQuery CRUD writes to activity_log only.
SearchAnalyticsEvent does NOT write to activity_log or audits — query volume is telemetry, not audit material. Would drown both streams.

notes: `SearchSyncJob.params` snapshots the request DTO; may contain filter values that are PII (e.g. `filter[email]=user@example.com`). Confidential.
`SearchSyncJob.last_error_message` may quote row values from failed operations (e.g. 'row 42: could not index athlete `alice@example.com`'). Confidential.
`SearchSynonym.terms` reveals workspace business terminology (product names, medical jargon, internal codes). Confidential. NEVER emit in cross-workspace contexts.

`SearchAnalyticsEvent.query` is the raw text of a user's search. Confidential + hidden on the wire (see schema `x-wire.hidden`). Retained only for `retention.analytics_query_days` (default 30 short / 90 medium / 365 long), then hard-purged. `query_hash` remains for aggregation.
`SearchAnalyticsEvent.query_hash` is SHA-256 of normalised query. NOT hidden — aggregation-safe (can't be reversed to raw text).
IP addresses of query originators are NOT stored. Search analytics is not audit evidence; keeping IPs would trigger GDPR IP-address requirements without a proportionate use case.

DOCUMENT CONTENT in physical engine indexes (Meilisearch, PG FTS, pgvector) inherits the source entity's classification. Enforcement is done by `HasSearchable::toSearchableArray()` — restricted-tier fields NEVER indexed. When an entity contains regulated_minor rows, indexing gates on VPC status per foundation's data-classes taxonomy.

`user::UserErased` triggers the AnonymiseSearchForErasedUser listener which nulls `SearchAnalyticsEvent.user_id` (GDPR Art. 17) and cascades ownership on `SearchSavedQuery` per the retention rules. Saved queries owned by an erased user are transferred to a workspace admin if `is_shared` is true; otherwise soft-deleted.
